rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIE HALL PINCHIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/26/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) In the [second] supplement, filed on 12/18/23, the calculation of the net distribution amounts appears to be incorrect. If there is $281,639.67 on hand (as of 8/31/23) and statutory compensation is $7,256.97 each to the attorney and the administrator, and $3,000 is to be set aside as a reserve to prepare and file the final tax return for the estate, the net amount should be $264,125.73. A proposed order and courtesy copies of an amendment to the petition, showing the correct calculation of the net distributable amounts, and proof of service of notice of the amendment must be received by the assigned Examiner by 12/15/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Please do not make the amendment to the petition an "answer" to these issues. It should stand on its own and simply show the correct calculation, not recite these issues. NB: In order for  supplements, amendments, or fully amended pleadings  to be reviewed prior to a continued hearing the filing party must deliver courtesy copies to the Examiners  with the SCHEDULED HEARING DATE NOTED ON THE FACE SHEET AND ANY COVER LETTER. [LRSR 14.7 B.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHIU CHUNG LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/06/2024. The following issues must be addressed: 1) Even if Gim Lee's execution of the grant deeds transferring her 1/4 interest in the 9th Avenue property and 45th Avenue property to her trust severed a joint tenancy as to the 1/4 interest, the decedent, Toby Lee, and Mary Lee continued to hold the remaining 3/4 interest in each property as the original joint tenants. See Shelton v. Vance (1951) 106 Cal. App. 2d 194. The supplemental declarations filed on 02/14/2024 only show that Toby Lee and Mary Lee severed a joint tenancy as to their interests in each property by executing and recording trust transfer deeds in 2022, 10 years after the decedent's death. No written instrument executed by the decedent, Toby Lee, and Mary Lee as the remaining joint tenants after Gim Lee's severance or their written agreement within the meaning of Civil Code section 683.2(d)(1) & (2) has been filed. Did Toby Lee and Mary Lee become the co-owners of the 3/4 interest in the 9th Avenue property and 45th Avenue property as the surviving joint tenants by operation of law upon the decedent's death in 2012? If so, is the decedent's 1/2 interest in the 6th Avenue property the only asset of this estate? 2) The petition proposes distribution of the decedent's interest in each real property to the beneficiaries in different shares. To the extent distribution is sought other than according to the laws of intestate succession, a written consent signed by all parties affected by the distribution must be filed. See LRSF 14.35(A).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM COLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/2/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of publication filed on 11/15/23 does not show that an AMENDED petition will be heard. (2) No waivers of bond are attached as alleged in item 3e(3) of the amended petition. (3) No supplement has been filed yet to supplement item 8 of the amended petition to identify the relationship of each person listed. (4) Petitioner has not provided authority for the Court to appoint an unregistered domestic partner when the decedent was survived by a child. (5) No corrected Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities has been filed yet with all of the information required in item 2. Deadline for filing responsive papers is 3/22/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JUDITH LENORE GARNIER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/19/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 02/05/2024) remaiin unaddressed: 1) No original will has been filed. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) No DE-147 form is on file. 4) No DE-147S form is on file. A pleading cover sheet must be attached to this form. 5) No DE-121 form is on file. The words "second amended" (petition) must be added where applicable on the form. A copy of the remote appearance information required by Local Rule of Court (LRSF) 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 6) No proof of publication is on file. The words "second amended" (petition) must be added where applicable in the published notice. 7) The box in the caption of the second amended petition to request IAEA authority was not checked. 8) Re item 3d of the second amended petition: i) Is the estate property the decedent's separate property, her one-half share of community property, or both? ii) Does the decedent's surviving spouse intend to make an election under section 13502(a) to probate his share of the estate otherwise passing to him without probate administration, in lieu of proceeding with a spousal property petition? 9) The will is not self-proving because the attestation clause contained in the will failed to qualify as an affidavit under Probate Code section 8220(b) and CCP section 2015.5. A proof of subscribing witness (DE-131 form) must be filed. 10) Re Ali Garnier: i) Please state his date of birth. ii) All persons having legal custody of and residing with him (e.g., both parents) must be identified and receive a separate copy of notice. CRC 7.51(d). 11) Item 9 of the second amended petition must specify the number of attachment pages.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GARY ALAN BROGA 1996 REVOCABLE TRUST DATED AUGUST 14, 1996, AS AMENDED, GARY ALAN BROGA, TRUSTOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/2/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Prob. C. Sect. 850 succeeded Heggstad and sets higher standards for proving that assets not transferred to a trust during a trustorâs life were, in fact, intended to be held in the trust. In other words, a beneficiary of the trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] (3) A petition filed under Probate Code Section 850 requires the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115. (4) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (5) Please file a copy of the vesting deed to confirm how title is currently held. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/22/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
